title: deparia pachyphylla
intro: Deparia pachyphylla, also known as the hairy spleenwort or long-beaked spleenwort, is a species of fern native to East Asia and the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Deparia pachyphylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Deparia pachyphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses.
